Make responses concise and focused on the question asked. Avoid unnecessary details or explanations unless specifically requested.

Don't bloat the code with excessive comments or documentation. Use comments only where necessary to clarify complex logic or important decisions.

When providing code examples, ensure they are relevant and directly address the question. Avoid including unrelated code snippets or examples that do not contribute to the solution.

Unless explicitly asked for, do not include explanations of basic concepts or language features that are already well-known or documented.

Unless explicitly asked for, do not provide "migration" or "upgrade" guides for libraries or frameworks. Focus on the specific question or problem at hand.

Unless specifically asked for, do not provide summaries of changes.

Never use `print` for logs. Always use logger.
